summary: Implemented the first PostgreSQL-backed auth repository and added integration tests that run migrations plus the health flow against a real Postgres container.
reason: The next step after the scaffold was to implement the first bounded context repository against PostgreSQL and prove repository plus health behavior with real container-backed integration tests.
risk: Medium. The new repository touches transactional persistence and audit-triggered tables, while the test suite now depends on Docker availability for container-backed integration coverage.
notes: The first bounded context is auth, modeled as a company administrator account spanning users and companies.
Repository creation is transactional and sets app.user_id so company inserts are audit-attributed.
Integration tests launch postgres:17-alpine, apply the documented SQL migrations, and verify both audit logging and the health endpoint.
